vllm.title: [CI/Build] Update vision tests
vllm.author: DarkLight1337
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This PR moves the LLaVA-specific test code into the model test file. It also cleans up some of the code in preparation for #4199.

vllm.title: Bugfix: fix broken of download models from modelscope
vllm.author: liuyhwangyh

vllm.title: [Kernel] Retune Mixtral 8x22b configs for FP8 on H100
vllm.author: pcmoritz
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This retunes the MoE configurations for Mixtral 8x22b with FP8 on H100
Improvements over existing configs (all measurements with 1000 input tokens and 50 output tokens):
TP4:
qps = 1: 14.4 ms (was 23.3 ms)
qps = 2: 17.1 ms (was 19.4 ms)
qps = 4: 28.7 ms (was 28.8 ms)
qps = 6: 42.3 ms (was 42.9 ms)

vllm.title: [Frontend] enable passing multiple LoRA adapters at once to generate()
vllm.author: mgoldey
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Following on the work in #3570, I see value in condensing multiple invocations of generate() to optimize batching and throughput when a number of LoRAs are in use.
As such, this PR attempts to enable passing multiple LoRAs at once to the generate invocation, so long as the length of LoRAs to be appl

vllm.title: [Core] Avoid copying prompt/output tokens if no penalties are used
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Copying those large tensors adds a non-trivial overhead. This is just a workaround - the proper fix would be to use a kernel for penalties that doesn't require tensors with all prompt/output tokens.

Thank you for your contribution to vLLM! Before submitting the pul

vllm.title: [Core] Change LoRA embedding sharding to support loading methods
vllm.author: Yard1
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Implements the logic outlined in #4997 (comment) to fix multi-LoRA used together with sharded state loader. After this change, it is possible to use a sharded state saved from a base model (without enable_lora=True) with a multi-LoRA enabled model.

vllm.title: [Misc] Missing error message for custom ops import
vllm.author: DamonFool
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Hi all,
The error message is missing if there is something wrong with the custom ops import.
I had spent several hours to debug a bug called NameError: name 'vllm_cache_ops' is not defined today.
Finally, I got the reason (a bad _C.cpython-310-x86_64-linux-gnu.so compiled).
However, if we had dump t

vllm.title: [Feature][Frontend]: Add support for `stream_options` in `ChatCompletionRequest`
vllm.author: Etelis
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR introduces support for the stream_options parameter in the ChatCompletionRequest class, addressing the feature request in issue #4967.
Changes

Added the StreamOptions class with an optional include_usage attribute.
Modified the ChatCompletionRequest class to include the stream_options attri

vllm.title: [Misc][Utils] allow get_open_port to be called for multiple times
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
#4914 allows users to specify a port to use, but is restricted to one port. When we want to use multiple ports (e.g. multiple LLM instances in offline inference for interactive playing), it will cause a problem.
This PR makes it possible to call get_open_port multiple times, and return different por
